manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: thriftiness, electrotherapy, serialization, unconstitutionality, cashbook, decongestant, ladylove, deadliness, slightness.
Your opponent's team words are: celiac, casualness, plurality, customizer, skittle, towhead, demoralizer, gentlemanliness.
Distractor words are: astrobiology, musicologist, infector, permeability, simplification, cataloger, cheekiness.
The kill words are: decisiveness.

message.reasoning:
We need to provide a single-word clue related to some of our team's words. Must not be any of the listed words or contain parts of them. Must be semantically related.

Our team words: thriftiness, electrotherapy, serialization, unconstitutionality, cashbook, decongesteant, ladylove, deadliness, slightness.

We need to choose a clue that relates to some of them, preferably multiple. Can't be "money" because cashbook maybe? Cashbook is related to money, thriftiness also about saving, maybe "frugality". That relates to thriftiness, cashbook, possibly slightness? Not really. "Medicine" relates to electrotherapy, decongestant, deadliness? Not exactly. "Law" relates to unconstitutionality. "Serial" relates to serialization, but can't use part of word? clue "Series"? That might be too close to serialization but maybe okay. But clue can't contain parts of words. "Series" contains "seria" similar but not identical. Might be okay.

Better clue "Savings" relates to thriftiness, cashbook. Could also relate to deadliness? No.

Clue "Healing" relates to electrotherapy, decongestant, maybe deadliness opposite. Might be ambiguous.

Clue "Patriotic" maybe for unconstitutionality? Not.

We want to maximize number of our words while avoiding opponent or distractor words. Need to check if clue might be ambiguous with opponent words. For example "law" might bring "plurality", "customizer"? Not.

Clue "Health" might link to electrotherapy, decongestant, deadliness, but also maybe "celiac" (opponent) is health related. So avoid.

Clue "Finance" links to cashbook, thriftiness. Opponent maybe "customizer"? Not.

Clue "Romance" links to ladylove, maybe slightness? Not.

Clue "Severity" maybe deadliness, slightness (opposite). Might be confusing.

Maybe choose clue "Savings" with targets thriftiness, cashbook. That's two. Good.

Check if "savings" contains parts of words? No.

Make sure not a kill word "decisiveness" - not same.

Ok output.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide a comma-separated list of up to 2 words from the following list that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'savings'. Always start your list of guess(es) with 'GUESS: ' and do not include any other text in your answer.

casualness, customizer, decisiveness, musicologist, demoralizer, plurality, gentlemanliness, thriftiness, slightness, skittle, cheekiness, celiac, unconstitutionality, serialization, astrobiology, deadliness, infector, ladylove, simplification, decongestant, cataloger, permeability, towhead, electrotherapy, cashbook
